steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.Pater: 'Pater' is a noun in the vocative — direct address to the Father
hints.spiritum: 'Spiritum' is a noun meaning 'spirit' in the accusative

hints.to praise: 'To praise' is 'laudare/commendare' can mean 'to praise', but here the primary sense is 'to entrust'

steps.reveal: commendo = I entrust / I commend (1st sing. pres.)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'commendem' — 'commendo' is indicative

hints.committo, committere, commisi, commissum: These are the parts of 'committo' (to join/entrust battle)
hints.comparo, comparare, comparavi, comparatu: These are the parts of 'comparo' (to prepare/compare)
hints.mando, mandare, mandavi, mandatum: These are the parts of 'mando' (to command/entrust) — 'commendare' is com- + mandare

hints.nominative: Nominative 'Pater' looks the same, but context shows direct address
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'Patris' — 'Pater' is vocative here

steps.reveal: in manus tuas = into your hands (acc. pl.)

hints.nominative singular: 'Manus' nom. sing. looks the same, but 'in' + accusative = motion into
hints.genitive singular: Genitive singular 'manus' also looks the same (4th decl.), but 'in' governs acc. here
hints.nominative plural: Nominative plural 'manus' looks the same, but this is acc. pl. after 'in' (motion)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'manus' has genitive in -us
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'manus' has genitive in -us
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'manus' has genitive in -us

steps.target: 'in' + accusative expresses motion toward — the spirit is being placed into the hands
choices: 'in' + accusative expresses motion toward — the spirit is being placed into the hands

hints.'in' always takes accusative: 'In' takes ablative for location, accusative for motion — here motion is intended
hints.'manus' is actually ablative here: Ablative plural of 'manus' (4th decl.) would be 'manibus' — 'manus' is acc. pl.
hints.it is a scribal error: This is correct Vulgate Latin — 'in manus tuas' with accusative expressing motion

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'spiritus' — 'spiritum' with -um is accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'spiritus' has genitive in -us
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'spiritus' has genitive in -us (4th decl.)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'spiritus' has genitive in -us

hints.nominative neuter singular: 'Meum' as nom. neut. is possible, but here it agrees with 'spiritum' (acc. masc.)
hints.genitive plural: Genitive plural would be 'meorum' — 'meum' is acc. masc. sing.
hints.ablative masculine singular: Ablative masculine would be 'meo' — 'meum' ends in -um (accusative)

translation: Father, into your hands I commend my spirit
